analysis.overall_assessment: Your data shows generally strong autonomic function with excellent average HRV (67ms) and low resting HR (46.7 bpm), suggesting good POTS stability. However, the dramatic HRV crash on 4/13 (41ms) following poor sleep (5.5h, 0.5h deep) demonstrates the critical sleep-autonomic connection for your conditions. Temperature stability (-0.0°C average) indicates well-controlled Hashimoto's activity currently.
whats_going_well: Excellent HRV resilience with rapid recovery from 41ms on 4/13 back to 82ms on 4/12, showing strong autonomic adaptability
Consistently low resting heart rate averaging 46.7 bpm indicates well-controlled POTS with no tachycardic episodes
Strong sleep efficiency at 86.6% and good sleep scores (81-86 on most nights) supporting autonomic recovery
areas_to_improve: Deep sleep quantity needs attention - 4/13 showed only 0.5h deep sleep coinciding with HRV crash, aim for consistent 1.2-1.5h nightly for immune system repair
Sleep duration consistency - the 5.5h night on 4/13 created cascading autonomic dysfunction, prioritize 7+ hours nightly
Activity pacing on higher step days like 4/11 (8068 steps) - monitor next-day HRV response to ensure not triggering POTS flares
analysis.autonomic_assessment: Your autonomic nervous system shows excellent baseline function with HRV consistently in the 60-80ms range and stable low resting HR. The 4/13 episode (HRV 41ms after poor sleep) demonstrates your system's vulnerability to sleep disruption but also shows impressive recovery capacity, returning to 82ms within one day with adequate rest.
analysis.thyroid_immune_signals: Temperature deviations remain stable at 0.0°C average, suggesting no current Hashimoto's flare activity. Deep sleep averaging 1.2h is adequate for immune regulation, though the 0.5h deep sleep on 4/13 may have temporarily compromised immune function.
analysis.vestibular_readiness: High readiness scores (84-88 on most days) indicate good capacity for vestibular rehab exercises. However, scale back vestibular training intensity on days following poor sleep like 4/14, when readiness dropped to 72 after the 4/13 sleep disruption.
analysis.focus_for_next_week: Prioritize sleep consistency by implementing a strict 9:30 PM wind-down routine to protect the 7+ hour sleep window that maintains your HRV above 60ms and prevents autonomic dysfunction cascades.
